name: Edward Beeching
bio: Edward Beeching is a Research Scientist at Hugging Face in Lyon, France, specializing in reinforcement learning for LLM alignment and embodied learning. He holds a PhD from INSA Lyon / INRIA CHROMA on deep RL for robotic navigation and planning. At Hugging Face he co-created the Open LLM Leaderboard, co-authored the Zephyr alignment model and the Alignment Handbook, integrated the Decision Transformer into the Transformers library, and co-authored TRL (Transformer Reinforcement Learning). He built Godot RL Agents (1,400 stars), an open-source framework connecting the Godot game engine to deep RL algorithms. His recent work includes SmolLM3, a 3B dual-mode reasoning model, the JAT multi-purpose transformer agent, and a survey of 16 open-source async RL training libraries. He has published 381 models and 103 datasets on Hugging Face.

timeline.event: Co-authored 'Keep the Tokens Flowing: Lessons from 16 Open-Source RL Libraries', a comprehensive survey of async RL training infrastructure
Co-authored SmolLM3, a 3B parameter dual-mode reasoning model with multilingual and 128k context support
Co-authored 'Optimizing Test-Time Compute via Meta Reinforcement Fine-Tuning' (ICML 2025)
Co-authored 'Jack of All Trades, Master of Some, a Multi-Purpose Transformer Agent' (JAT) — first fully open-sourced multi-task RL agent
Co-authored 'Zephyr: Direct Distillation of LM Alignment' — 7B chat model surpassing Llama2-Chat-70B on MT-Bench without human annotation
Co-created the Open LLM Leaderboard on Hugging Face to track open-source LLM progress
Co-authored 'StackLLaMA: A hands-on guide to train LLaMA with RLHF' blog post on Hugging Face
Defended PhD thesis 'Large-scale automatic learning of autonomous agent behavior with structured deep reinforcement learning' at INSA Lyon
Published Decision Transformer integration in Hugging Face Transformers library
Joined Hugging Face as a Research Scientist in Lyon
Published 'Learning to Plan with Uncertain Topological Maps' at ECCV 2020 (spotlight)

key_contributions.title: Godot RL Agents
key_contributions.description: Open-source framework connecting the Godot game engine to deep RL algorithms (StableBaselines3, Sample Factory, Ray RLLib, CleanRL). 1,400 stars on GitHub.

key_contributions.title: Open LLM Leaderboard
key_contributions.description: Co-created the Hugging Face Open LLM Leaderboard for benchmarking open-source large language models on IFEval, BBH, MATH, GPQA, MUSR, and MMLU-PRO

key_contributions.title: Zephyr: Direct Distillation of LM Alignment
key_contributions.description: Co-authored Zephyr-7B, a distilled chat model that surpassed Llama2-Chat-70B on MT-Bench using distilled direct preference optimization without human annotation

key_contributions.title: The Alignment Handbook
key_contributions.description: Co-authored robust recipes for aligning language models with human and AI preferences, including fine-tuning recipes for SmolLM2-Instruct and Zephyr

key_contributions.title: TRL (Transformer Reinforcement Learning)
key_contributions.description: Co-author of Hugging Face's open-source library for training transformer language models with RL techniques including SFT, GRPO, and DPO

key_contributions.title: Decision Transformer in Transformers
key_contributions.description: Integrated the Decision Transformer model into Hugging Face's transformers library, enabling offline RL via conditional sequence modeling

key_contributions.title: SmolLM3
key_contributions.description: Co-authored SmolLM3, a 3B parameter dual-mode reasoning model with multilingual support, 128k context, and state-of-the-art performance at 3B scale

key_contributions.title: JAT: Jack of All Trades Multi-Purpose Transformer Agent
key_contributions.description: Co-authored the first fully open-sourced multi-task RL agent capable of sequential decision-making, CV, and NLP with a single set of weights

key_contributions.title: Mixture-of-Thoughts Dataset
key_contributions.description: Published the Mixture-of-Thoughts dataset on Hugging Face with 349k items and 197 likes, used for reasoning model training

quotes.text: I had the opportunity to spend the last month building an open-source, state of the art, dual-mode reasoning model at the 3B scale. Building on the amazing work of Hugging Face's Pretraining team. It was tough but we managed to get on the Pareto front with the Qwen3 models.

A month ago I joined Hugging Face as a Research Scientist. They're great: opening an office in Lyon, allowing me to work on open-source projects and trusting me to define my own schedule. I am proud to have added the Decision Transformer to transformers.
quotes.source: X (Twitter) post on joining Hugging Face

quotes.text: For those of you who don't have time to read 5,000 words about async RL plumbing (we get it, you have models to train).
quotes.source: Keep the Tokens Flowing blog post
